codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of wallet connection UI with ERC-6963 discovery, window.ethereum fallback, connect/disconnect flows, sender auto-fill, and provider deduplication by UUID. The code is well-structured with proper error handling (including EIP-4001 user rejection), accessible UI (aria-live on message area), responsive CSS, and graceful image error fallbacks. All 5 targeted validation items (VAL-WALLET-001 through 004, 010) are covered. Downstream features successfully consume the wallet state variables (connectedWalletProvider, connectedWalletAddressValue) for swap transactions and auto-refresh sender inclusion.

issues.description: No accountsChanged event listener on the connected provider. If a user switches accounts in their wallet extension, the UI will still show the old address and the sender field won't update. Consider adding: provider.on('accountsChanged', (accounts) => { ... }) after successful connection.

window.__selectedWallet* globals are set but never consumed — downstream features (swap-transaction-buttons, auto-refresh-quotes) directly reference the local connectedWalletProvider/connectedWalletAddressValue variables since they share the same script scope. The globals are dead code unless intended for external consumers (e.g., browser console debugging). Not harmful but unnecessary.

Wallet integration tests only check that the HTML source contains expected string literals (element IDs, event names). They don't test any behavioral flow. Acceptable given the inline-template architecture, but the test names ('shows a Connect Wallet button', 'includes ERC-6963 discovery') could be more precise about what they actually verify (HTML content presence).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The wallet-connection feature introduces a pattern of sharing client-side state between features via module-scoped variables (connectedWalletProvider, connectedWalletAddressValue, connectedWalletInfo) plus window.__selectedWallet* globals. This cross-feature state-sharing convention isn't documented in AGENTS.md or .factory/library/architecture.md, but downstream features (swap-transaction-buttons, auto-refresh-quotes) depend on these exact variable names.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.ts:468-470 defines the variables, lines 1152,1673,1749,1786 (added by later features) consume them directly. window.__selectedWallet* globals at lines 571-573 are set but never read by other features.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'Browser-side wallet interaction uses viem's createWalletClient + custom transport' but the worker correctly chose raw EIP-1193 provider.request() calls instead, which the fullstack-worker skill explicitly permits as the simpler option. The AGENTS.md guidance is stale/misleading for the connect flow — viem walletClient is only used later for sendTransaction/writeContract in the swap feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md Key Technical Context section shows viem walletClient pattern; .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md step 3 says 'Use raw EIP-1193 provider.request() calls (simpler, no external deps)'; worker used provider.request({ method: 'eth_requestAccounts' }) at src/server.ts:644

summary: PASS. The wallet-connection feature cleanly implements ERC-6963 provider discovery with window.ethereum fallback, connect/disconnect UI flows, sender auto-fill, and UUID-based provider deduplication. All 5 targeted validation items are satisfied. Three non-blocking issues noted: missing accountsChanged listener (users switching accounts in-wallet won't update UI), unused window.__selectedWallet* globals, and integration tests that only verify HTML content rather than behavior. Two shared-state observations: undocumented cross-feature variable naming convention and stale AGENTS.md guidance about viem browser usage.
